title: Intensifiers: Rất, Lắm, Quá
description: Learn when to use rất (very, formal), lắm (very, casual), and quá (too/so, emotional)
intro: Vietnamese has three main intensifiers: 'rất' goes BEFORE adjectives (formal), 'lắm' goes AFTER (casual), and 'quá' expresses strong emotion (too/so!). They look interchangeable but have strict placement rules and different nuances.

questions.prompt: Where does 'rất' go in a sentence?
options: At the end of the sentence
questions.explanation: 'rất' always goes BEFORE: 'rất lạnh' (very cold), 'rất nhanh' (very fast).

questions.prompt: Where does 'lắm' go in a sentence?
questions.explanation: 'lắm' goes AFTER: 'tốt lắm' (very good), 'nhanh lắm' (very fast).

questions.prompt: Ở bên ngoài ___ lạnh. (It's VERY cold outside.)
questions.explanation: 'rất' before 'lạnh': 'rất lạnh'. This is a commentary sentence, so 'rất' fits best.

questions.prompt: Trường học đó tốt ___. (That school is VERY good.)
Em đi chậm ___! (You walk TOO slowly!)
questions.explanation: 'quá' as an exclamation: 'chậm quá!' = too slow! Expresses the speaker's frustration.
You CAN say 'không...lắm' (not very) but CANNOT say 'rất không...' — that's ungrammatical.

questions.prompt: Tôi chơi thể thao không giỏi ___. (I'm NOT VERY good at sports.)
questions.explanation: 'không...lắm' = not very. 'không giỏi lắm' = not very good. Can't use 'rất' in negation.

questions.prompt: When 'quá' comes BEFORE an adjective, what does it often imply?
questions.explanation: 'quá mắc' = too expensive (leads to negative consequence: can't buy it). Pre-position 'quá' often implies something bad.

questions.prompt: 'Cà phê Việt Nam đậm lắm' vs 'Cà phê Việt Nam đậm quá!' — what's the difference?
questions.explanation: 'đậm lắm' = talking about something already experienced. 'đậm quá!' = reacting in the moment.

questions.prompt: Cô nói tiếng Việt ___ hay! (You speak Vietnamese SO well!)
questions.explanation: 'quá' here is an emotional exclamation. 'hay quá!' or 'quá hay!' = So good!
'rất' is used in formal speech and writing. 'lắm' is more casual/conversational. 'quá' is exclamatory.

questions.prompt: ___ có thể là hai công ty ấy sẽ đóng cửa. (There is a VERY high possibility...)
questions.explanation: 'rất' can also go before modal verbs like 'có thể' (can/possible): 'rất có thể' = very possibly.
You can say 'không...lắm' but NOT 'không...quá'. 'Không xa lắm' = not very far (correct).

questions.prompt: Quyển từ điển này hay nhưng ___ mắc, tôi mua không nổi. (This dictionary is good but TOO expensive...)
questions.explanation: 'quá mắc' = too expensive, leading to consequence (can't buy). 'quá' before adj = negative connotation.

questions.prompt: Tỷ lệ sinh ở các nước phát triển ___ thấp. (Birth rates in developed countries are VERY low.)
questions.explanation: Formal/factual statement about statistics → 'rất' (before adj). Commentary style.

questions.prompt: Arrange from most to least intense: rất, quá, lắm
questions.explanation: 'quá' is the strongest (emotional), 'rất' and 'lắm' are similar in intensity but differ in formality and position.
